name: pyjson5

metadata.name: pyjson5
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: JSON5 serializer and parser for Python 3 written in Cython.

documentation: # PyJSON5 A JSON5 serializer and parser library for Python 3 written in [Cython](http://cython.org/). ## Serializer The serializer returns ASCII data that can safely be used in an HTML template. Apostrophes, ampersands, greater-than, and less-then signs are encoded as unicode escaped sequences. E.g. this snippet is safe for any and all input: ```html "show message" ``` Unless the input contains infinite or NaN values, the result will be valid [JSON](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc8259) data. ## Parser All valid [JSON5 1.0.0](https://spec.json5.org/) and [JSON](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc8259) data can be read, unless the nesting level is absurdly high. ## Functions You can find the full documentation online at . Or simply call `help(pyjson5)`. :-) The library supplies load(s) and dump(s) functions, so you can use it as a drop-in replacement for Python's builtin `json` module, but you *should* use the functions `encode_*()` and `decode_*()` instead. ## Compatibility At least CPython 3.5 or a recent Pypy3 version is needed.
